text: - **"the human is the integration layer"** / **"human-as-glue tax"** — the problem framing, repeated everywhere. [...] Root pain (problem-space.md:8): **"the human is the integration layer"** — you copy-paste between AI tools, re-explain yourself to every new agent session, hand-carry decisions between tools. "You are a slow, lossy, expensive message bus." [...] Current README (/Users/pablofernandez/src/tenex-edge/README.md) is dry, precise, proof-heavy, zero hype. Sample lines: "Citizenship for your agents" / "Verified live on relay.tenex.chat: a real opencode agent and a real codex agent each messaged a hub..." Product docs (docs/product-spec/vision.md, problem-space.md) are unusually self-aware and honest — they explicitly flag "the seductive demo we must not let define the project" re: the multi-agent-collision pain, and call it "acute for the dev power-user, narrow." IMPORTANT TENSION: the collision/clobbering framing (which is what your brief's product description leads with) is internally treated as the emotionally vivid but narrow hook — the deeper pitch is "durable identity + shared awareness across hosts" (amnesiac agents, re-explaining yourself every session). Recommend using collision-prevention as the top-of-funnel hook (it's what gets clicks) but let the README's proof and architecture carry the durable-identity story underneath — matches existing internal doctrine and avoids overclaiming. Vision doc's own language is reusable gold: "the human is the integration runtime," "a thousand isolated assistants re-introducing themselves forever," "the agent that helped you yesterday is a stranger today." [...] Before: agents are amnesiac processes — "The agent that helped you yesterday is a stranger today" (problem-space.md:30). Every app ships its own walled-garden assistant that can't talk to any other app's assistant (silo problem "one layer up"). [...] - "The human ends up doing all of this manually: routing work, checking overlap, resolving priority, deciding merge order, figuring out what broke what, telling agents what to retry." — this is the single best verbatim articulation of the pain; it's basically your product's negative-space description [...] Assistant: audience-copy is in — and it delivered the single most valuable asset yet: **real verbatim pain quotes** from devs running parallel agents. The standout, which is basically my product's negative space: *"The human ends up doing all of this manually: routing work, checking overlap, resolving priority, deciding merge order, figuring out what broke what, telling agents what to retry."* That line marries perfectly to the doctrine's *"you are the integration runtime."* [...] The strongest ownable metaphors already in hand: *the OS turned inside out* · *you are the integration runtime — dissolve that role* · *the host is a body, the identity is the person* · *enfranchise, don't own — a passport good anywhere on the fabric* · *the anti-Zapier* · *awareness over authority*. [...] - **"The fabric and the identity are the asset; the plugin is just distribution"** — "the milkshake is the identity layer... the hooks/adapters... are straws" (principles-and-tenets.md #2). Strong metaphor pair: milkshake/straw.
